sn47.12:1.1: This was apparently the last time that the Buddha met Sāriputta. This encounter is recorded as part of the Mahāparinibbānasutta at DN 16:1.61.1 and expanded into a long discourse at DN 28. | Here in the Saṁyutta, this meeting is followed with the record of Sāriputta’s passing at SN 47.13, and then the reflection on the absence of both Sāriputta and Moggallāna at SN 47.14 . For some reason, these events were omitted from the Mahāparinibbānasutta, even though they fit well thematically. The passing of the great disciples gives a chance for the Buddha to show the equanimous wisdom with which such tragedies should be met, in contrast with Ānanda’s emotional fragility. Other themes, such as the practice of mindfulness to be an island for oneself, the support for the fourfold assembly, the key role of a carer, or the pursuit of excellence also fit well with the Mahāparinibbānasutta.
sn47.12:6.2: As at DN 14:1.37.4, inference (anvaya) is regarded as a valid form of knowledge. The Buddha himself is said to rely on inference using the same simile at AN 10.95:10.4. See SN 12.33 and notes for discussion.
sn47.12:7.2: This closing section is not found in DN 16, but is at DN 28:21.2.
